models.model_label: UFO (Unified Foundational Ontology) - UFO-A, UFO-B, UFO-C

evidence_quotes.quote: In this paper, we illustrated the role played by philosophically  grounded foundational ontology in the design of domain  ontologies. In particular, we demonstrate how UFO can be used to evaluate, re-design and give real-world semantics to an ontology in the software engineering domain, namely, the software process ontology which is the core of the ODE Ontology-Centered Software Development Environment.

By doing this, we have corrected a number of con- ceptual problems in this software engineering ontology by making it more truthful to the  domain  being  represented  and  by  making  explicit  its  ontological  commitments.

goals.justification: UFO is used as an analytical instrument to evaluate and identify shortcomings in another ontology (ODE), matching the Problem-solving signal patterns 'evaluate' and 'identify shortcomings'. The model guides decision-making about what needs to change in the target ontology.

evidence_quotes.quote: Foundational  Ontologies are  theoretically  well-founded  domainindependent systems of categories that have been successfully used to improve the quality of conceptual modeling languages and models.

UFO has been successfully employed  to  evaluate,  re-design  and  integrate  the  models  of  conceptual  modeling languages  as  well  as  to  provide  real-world  semantics  for  their  modeling  constructs

goals.justification: UFO is used to provide 'real-world semantics' and clarify the meaning of modeling constructs in domain ontologies, fitting the Understanding signal patterns 'clarify' and 'conceptualize'.

evidence_quotes.quote: In  [16],  Falbo  and  Bertollo  presented  a  Software  Process  Ontology  that  was  developed for establishing a common conceptualization for software organizations to talk about  software  processes.

By doing this, we have corrected a number of con- ceptual problems in this software engineering ontology by making it more truthful to the  domain  being  represented  and  by  making  explicit  its  ontological  commitments.

goals.justification: The ODE ontology was explicitly developed for 'establishing a common conceptualization' - matching the Understanding signal pattern of 'conceptualize' and shared meaning negotiation about the software process domain.

evidence_quotes.quote: This ontology is used as basis for the development  of  a process  infrastructure  for  ODE  [3],  a  Process-Centered  Software  Engineering  Environment.

goals.justification: The ODE Software Process Ontology is explicitly stated to be 'used as basis for the development of a process infrastructure' - the ontology guides the construction of a concrete software environment, matching the Intervening signal 'used to create'.

evidence_quotes.quote: presented  a  Software  Process  Ontology  that  was  developed for establishing a common conceptualization for software organizations to talk about  software  processes.

uncertain_goals.reason: The phrase 'to talk about software processes' weakly suggests communication between organizations, but the primary framing is establishing a common conceptualization (Understanding) rather than explicitly supporting interactive communication between distinct parties.
